# DISPATCH-INVENTORY.md
## D0: Route-Coverage Inventory — Self-Hosted Compiler Core IR

**Generated by:** `selfhost/dispatch_inventory_main.mdk`  
**Pipeline:** `elaborateModules` (typed route-stamping) + `lowerGroups`/`lowerImplsWith` over all selfhost modules  
**Entry:** `selfhost/all_modules_entry.mdk` (pulls all 15 compiler modules transitively)

---

### SUMMARY TABLE

| Route Kind  | Count | Examples (method@tag)                               |
|-------------|-------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| RNone       | 1     | `empty@?`                                           |
| RKey-ground | 189   | `pure@Parser`, `andThen@Parser`, `foldRight@List`, `debug@Pat` |
| RKey-nested | 0     | —                                                   |
| RDict       | 2     | `pure@$dict_unless_0`, `pure@$dict_when_0`          |
| RDictFwd    | 0     | —                                                   |
| **CMethod total** | **192** | |
| CDict sites | 0     | (no `=>` constrained-function occurrences in Core IR) |
| ArgTag CVar (upper bound) | 1065 | `min`, `fold`, `max`, `andThen`, `display`, `hash`, `debug`, `eq`, `filterMap`, `foldRight`, `toList`, `ap` |

---

### PRIMITIVE vs ADT

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Primitive-typed RKey sites (tag ∈ Int/Float/String/Bool/Char/Array) | **0** |
| ADT-typed RKey sites | 189 |
| Max RKey nesting depth | **0** |

All 189 RKey dispatch sites target user/stdlib ADT types (`Parser`, `Result`, `List`,
`Option`, various AST/IR types). **Zero primitive-typed return-position dispatch** in
the self-hosted compiler.

---

### WOULD-PANIC IN CURRENT EMITTER

| Panic reason | Count |
|--------------|-------|
| RNone (arg-tag fallback, emitter panics) | 1 |
| RKey with nested reqs (`ensureNoReqs`) | 0 |
| Non-empty methRoutes/implRoutes (`ensureNoMethodDicts`) | 0 |
| **TOTAL would-panic** | **1** |

The single RNone site is `empty@?` — an `empty` interface method call whose
route the typechecker left unresolved. Everything else is either statically resolved
(RKey-ground) or routes through a dict param (RDict for `when`/`unless`).

---

### IMPL METHOD NAMES in scope (39 total)

`eq`, `append`, `empty`, `lt`, `gt`, `lte`, `gte`, `min`, `max`, `compare`,
`debug`, `display`, `hash`, `add`, `sub`, `mul`, `div`, `negate`, `abs`, `signum`,
`pure`, `andThen`, `fold`, `foldRight`, `toList`, `fromList`, `map`, `flatMap`,
`ap`, `filter`, `filterMap`, `singleton`, `insert`, `member`, `size`,
`contains`, `keys`, `values`, `union`

---

### ARG-TAG CVar OCCURRENCES (1065, upper bound)

Top names: `min`, `fold`, `max`, `andThen`, `display`, `hash`, `debug`, `eq`,
`filterMap`, `foldRight`, `toList`, `ap`

These are bare `CVar` nodes naming interface methods — they occur in
argument-position uses (passed to higher-order functions, used in let bindings)
where the typed pipeline does NOT replace them with `CMethod`. They fall back to
arg-tag dispatch in the evaluator. The 1065 count is an **upper bound** —
some of these occurrences may be used as unapplied higher-order values rather
than direct dispatch sites.

---

### SCOPE READOUT

The self-hosted compiler Core IR (post `elaborateModules` + `lowerGroups`) carries
**192 CMethod** dispatch sites and **0 CDict** sites.

**CMethod breakdown:** 189 RKey-ground, 0 RKey-nested (max depth 0), 2 RDict,
0 RDictFwd, 1 RNone.

**Primitive-typed RKey:** 0. **ADT-typed:** 189. All dispatch targets are
user/stdlib ADT types. Zero primitive-typed return-position dispatch.

**Would-panic sites:** 1 (1× RNone for `empty`). Everything else is emittable
with the current spike's RKey-direct and RDict if-chain paths.

**D1 scope:** No nested-requires dicts found (max depth = 0). D1 is ground-only:
RKey with empty `reqs`. No requires-chain materialization needed for the compiler.

**D2 scope:** 2 RDict sites (`pure` inside `when`/`unless` prelude helpers).
These need the runtime dict-param-passing path in the emitter.

**D3 scope:** 0 primitive-typed RKey sites in the typed return-position call set.
The arg-tag gap (CVar upper bound) is 1065 occurrences. These are argument-position
method uses (`eq`, `debug`, `fold`, etc.) that go through arg-tag dispatch.
The evaluator handles them; the emitter's `emitMethodArgDispatch` handles ADT-typed
ones already. Since all 189 RKey sites target ADT types, **retiring arg-tag
for primitive dispatch adds 0 currently-elaborated return-position sites** to the
emitter's workload. D3's actual work is either (a) stamping more argument-position
occurrences with routes, or (b) extending `emitMethodArgDispatch` to handle the
existing arg-tag population.

---

## D0.5: Arg-Position Dispatch Classification by Receiver Type-Class

**Generated by:** `selfhost/dispatch_argclass_main.mdk` (probe) + `typecheck.measureModules`
(measurement-only inference behind `argMeasureEnabled`; byte-identical with it OFF).
**Pipeline:** prePass return-position methods → EMethodAt, typecheck every module
in dependency order seeded like `checkModules`, **and also infer every impl method
body** (the golden `elaborateModules` path skips impl bodies; D0's Core-IR walk
included them, so this matches its population). Each arg-position interface-method
`EVar` that reaches `inferVar` is captured with the *inferred type of its
discriminating argument*, normalized after inference, and classified.

D0 left the 1065 arg-tag `CVar` population **untyped** (an upper bound). D0.5
resolves the one decision-driving number: of those occurrences, how many have a
**statically-known receiver** (concrete → stampable as a static `RKey`, the
emitter already handles `RKey`) vs a **type-variable receiver** (known only at the
polymorphic fn's call site → genuinely needs **dict-passing**).

---

### PRECISE RECOUNT

Total arg-position interface-method occurrences: **1013** (typed recount; D0's 1065
was a by-name `CVar` upper bound that also caught non-dispatch uses of impl-method
*names* — e.g. `size`/`member`/`keys` as ordinary identifiers — and unreached code).
**Operators are NOT in this set:** `==`/`<`/`+`/`++` lower to `CBinPrim` (primitives
the emitter already handles via `valueEq`/`valueCompare`), not method `CVar`s.

| Receiver type-class | Count | Emitter disposition |
|---------------------|-------|---------------------|
| concrete-primitive (Int/Float/String/Bool/Char/Array) | **25** | **cannot emit today** — primitive immediates carry no runtime tag; needs static-RKey stamping |
| concrete-ADT (user/stdlib ADT head) | **878** | already emittable (`emitMethodArgDispatch` handles ADT-typed; static RKey) |
| type-variable (unsolved/quantified tyvar) | **110** | **needs dict-passing** (type known only at the polymorphic call site) |
| other (Fun/Tuple discriminating arg) | 0 | — |

---

### HEADLINE

| Metric | Count |
|--------|-------|
| **statically-resolvable** (concrete → static RKey; front-end *marking* extension) | **903** |
| **genuinely-polymorphic** (type-variable → real *dict-passing* build) | **110** |

**~89% of the arg-position population is statically resolvable.** The dispatch lift
is **mostly a front-end marking extension**, not a dict-passing build — but
dict-passing is **NOT zero**: 110 occurrences genuinely need it.

---

### APPLICATION SHAPE (orthogonal overlay)

| Shape | Count |
|-------|-------|
| directly-applied (`EApp (EVar m) _`; dispatch arg supplied at the call) | **1013** |
| first-class / unapplied (method passed as a value, section, let-bound) | **0** |

**The "unapplied-value" bucket is empty.** The self-hosted compiler never passes an
interface method as a bare first-class value (no `map debug xs`, `foldr eq …`);
every dispatch occurrence is applied directly to its discriminating argument. So the
discriminating type is always determinable, and the concrete-vs-type-variable split
above is the whole story — there is no "knowable-from-context vs genuinely-polymorphic"
ambiguity to resolve within a first-class bucket.

---

### CONCRETE-PRIMITIVE breakdown (the 25 sites the spike CANNOT emit today)

| method @ head tycon | count |
|---------------------|-------|
| `display @ String` | 19 |
| `compare @ Int` | 3 |
| `compare @ String` | 2 |
| `compare @ Float` | 1 |

These are the *only* arg-position sites the current emitter cannot handle (primitive
receivers). All 25 are statically stampable as `RKey Int` / `RKey String` / etc. once
the marker is extended to arg-position concrete sites. (`display @ String` dominates:
`Display`/`Debug` impl bodies rendering string fields.)

Concrete-ADT heads: `Parser ×611` (do-notation desugars 612 `<-` binds in
`parser.mdk` to `andThen` on `Parser` — arg-position monad dispatch on a known ADT)
and `List ×267` (`map`/`filter`/`fold` over lists).

---

### TYPE-VARIABLE sites (the 110 genuine dict-passing sites) — WHERE THEY LIVE

| Enclosing fn | method | count |
|--------------|--------|-------|
| `Eq.eq` (parametric impl bodies) | `eq` | 18 |
| `Ord.compare` (parametric impl bodies) | `compare` | 18 |
| `Debug.debug` (parametric impl bodies) | `debug` | 17 |
| `Display.display` (parametric impl bodies) | `display` | 17 |
| `Hashable.hash` (parametric impl bodies) | `hash` | 17 |
| prelude generic helpers (`neq`, `clamp`, `debugListItems`, `displayListItems`, `hashListItems`, `any`/`all`/`find`/`count`/`sum`/`product`/`elem`/`maximum`/`minimum`, `flatMap`/`flat`, `print`/`println`) | mixed | ~23 |

Two distinct origins:
- **~87 in derived/parametric impl bodies** — `impl Eq (List a) requires Eq a` etc.,
  whose body dispatches the element method (`eq`/`compare`/`debug`/`hash`) on the
  impl's element tyvar `a`. These are exactly the Phase 83/84 `requires`-dict element
  dispatches, but in **argument** position (Phase 83/84 wired the *return*-position
  case as `RDict`; the arg-position element dispatch still falls to eval's arg-tag).
- **~23 in prelude generic constrained functions** — `Eq a => …`, `Ord a => …`,
  `Foldable`/`Debug` helpers — polymorphic in the dispatch type at their own call sites.

---

### SCOPE READOUT (D1–D3)

**Does the compiler need dict-passing for arg-position sites?** **YES, but only for
110 of 1013 (~11%).** The type-variable bucket is non-empty, so arg-position dispatch
is *not* entirely statically resolvable — D3 cannot be "marking only."

- **D3a (front-end marking, the bulk):** stamp the **903 concrete** arg-position sites
  (25 primitive + 878 ADT) with a static `RKey` by extending the marker to
  argument-position occurrences whose discriminating type resolves to a concrete head.
  The emitter already handles `RKey` (incl. the 25 primitive heads once stamped). This
  retires arg-tag for everything except true polymorphism.
- **D3b (dict-passing, the real-but-bounded work):** the **110 type-variable** sites
  need a dict threaded from the polymorphic call site. ~87 are derived-impl element
  dispatches (the `requires`-dict already exists for return-position — extend it to
  arg-position element methods); ~23 are prelude generic constrained fns (the same
  `=>`-dict-passing layer the typed pipeline already builds for return-position, applied
  to arg-position method bodies).
- **Monomorphization alternative:** if the backend specializes generic instances
  (`List Int` etc.) instead of dict-passing, most of the 110 collapse to concrete
  `RKey` at each specialization — i.e. the 110 are the sites whose disposition depends
  on the dict-passing-vs-monomorph decision (decided: dict-passing, monomorph deferred).

**Bottom line:** the arg-position dispatch lift is **concrete-heavy (~89% static
marking)** with a **small, well-localized dict-passing core (110 sites, all
directly-applied, concentrated in 5 derived-impl method bodies + ~18 prelude helpers)**.
No first-class/unapplied method values exist to complicate it.

---

## D3a: Concrete arg-position sites STAMPED → RKey (2026-06-07)

The 903 concrete arg-position sites (25 primitive + 878 ADT) are now stamped as static
`RKey <head>` on the LLVM **emit path** (gated behind `argStampEnabled`, off for every
golden/oracle driver so all goldens stay byte-identical). Implementation: `typecheck.mdk`
`prePassDictArg` (rewrite arg-position method `EVar`→`EMethodAt`), `inferMethodAt`→
`recordArgStamp` (queue the route ref keyed on the *discriminating-arg* mono),
`resolveArgStamps` (concrete head → `RKey`, type-variable → `RNone`), and `inferPlainImpl`
(infer NON-`requires` impl bodies on the emit path so the `display@String` impl-body sites
are reached). The emitter's existing `RKey` path emits primitive impl fns
(`@mdk_impl_Int_compare`, `@mdk_impl_String_display`) unchanged — **no emitter-side fix was
needed** beyond turning `emitMethod`'s `RNone` arm from a panic into the arg-tag fallback (so
the 110 unstamped type-variable sites keep working until D3b).

**The 25 primitive sites now emit.** Verified end-to-end by two fixtures that PANICKED before
D3a (`emitMethodArgDispatch` → "impl type owns no constructors": primitive immediates have no
cell tag) and pass after: `test/llvm_fixtures_typed/disp_arg_prim_compare.mdk` (`compare@Int`,
multi-impl so the arg-tag path is genuinely exercised) and `disp_arg_prim_display.mdk`
(`display@String` inside a `Display` impl body). The existing arg-position ADT fixtures
(`disp_arg_single`/`multi`/`clauses`) now also lower to static `RKey` (same output) — retiring
runtime arg-tag dispatch for concrete receivers.

**Type-variable count unchanged at 110** (D3b): `resolveArgStamps` leaves those `RNone`; the
emitter arg-tag-dispatches them. (Re-running the D0.5 probe post-D3a reports 25 primitive / 110
type-variable **unchanged** and concrete-ADT 878→880 / total 1013→1015 — the +2 is D3a's own
new code, which `all_modules_entry` analyzes: `map fst …` / `map snd …` calls in the added
`elaborateDict`/stamp helpers are themselves `map@List` arg-position sites. The classification
machinery is untouched.)

**The 1 RNone `empty` is still open** (deferred): `foldMap`'s default-body seed routes `RNone`
because the `elaborateModules` bootstrap path runs `implInferEnabled = False` and never infers
interface default bodies (the gate that keeps module goldens byte-identical). Folded into
D3b-2/D4 — see STAGE2-DESIGN §2.4c. It is not on the LLVM fixture path, so it blocks nothing now,
and D3a's `RNone → arg-tag` fallback means it no longer panics the emitter.

---

## D3b-1: derived-impl arg-position element dispatch → RDict requires-dict (2026-06-07)

The ~87 type-variable arg-position sites that live INSIDE parametric `requires` impl bodies
(`Eq.eq ×18`, `Ord.compare ×18`, `Debug.debug ×17`, `Display.display ×17`, `Hashable.hash ×17`
in the D0.5 table — the element method dispatched on the impl's element tyvar `a`) now route
through the impl's `requires`-dict (`RDict $dict_<method>_<slot>`) on the LLVM **emit path**,
instead of `RNone`→arg-tag. Arg-tag cannot resolve these when the element is a PRIMITIVE (an
`Int`/`Float`/`String` immediate carries no cell tag), so the native backend needs the dict.

Implementation (all gated behind `argStampEnabled`, off for every golden/oracle driver):
`typecheck.mdk` `registerImplRequires` registers the element dict var for arg-position dispatch
methods too (via `registersElementDict`); `resolveArgStamp` routes the element tyvar →
`RDict`, a concrete head → `RKey` while filling the impl-dicts ref via `argImplDictRoutesFor`
(so the outer/recursive `List` call threads the element dict — concrete element → `RKey Int`,
recursive element → `RDict` forward); `dictPass`'s `implDictPassMethods` prepends the leading
`$dict_<method>_<slot>` param uniformly across a dict-using method's clauses. The emitter's
`RKey` arm materializes the element-dict witnesses (`dictWordsOf`) as leading args; the
arg-position `RDict` site reuses the existing `emitMethodDispatch` dict-read.

Verified by two fixtures that PANICKED pre-D3b-1 (`emitMethodArgDispatch` → "impl type owns no
constructors" on the primitive element) and pass after: `disp_req_eq_list_int.mdk` (`eq` on
`MyList Int` via `Eq (MyList a) requires Eq a`) and `disp_req_compare_list_int.mdk`
(`compare` via `Ord (MyList a) requires Ord a`). One-level only (nested element dicts like
`MyList (MyList Int)` stay out of scope — the dict witness carries only the head tag).

**D0.5 probe re-run UNCHANGED:** 25 primitive / 110 type-variable / 880 ADT / 1015 total — the
probe classifies by discriminating-arg TYPE on the measurement path (stamping off), so the
type-variable bucket is unmoved; the `RNone`→`RDict` routing change is emit-path-only. **Of the
110: the ~87 derived-impl element dispatches now route `RDict` on the emit path (D3b-1 done);
the remaining ~23 prelude `=>` generic helpers + the 1 `empty` default-body seed stay `RNone` →
arg-tag (D3b-2).**
